metadata.title: Build a Boondoggle Score Calculator with Claude
metadata.purpose: Show students in Nina Harris' Branding and AI course how to build a Boondoggle Score calculator that decomposes any task into AI-does-best and human-conducts sub-steps, scores the seam placement, and flags misplaced judgment.
metadata.audience: Students and practitioners in a university branding course with an AI component
sources: Branding and AI, Chapter 97 — Fundamental Themes (Nina Harris)

skin_warnings: B00: palette=claude but the cold open is 'ClaudeComposerAsk916' — COLD OPEN LAW wants ClaudeComposerAsk
END: palette=claude but the outro is 'un-annotated' — OUTRO LAW wants ClaudeTitleOutro

metadata.reformat: center-cut, focus-aware; pantry/<bid>-916.* is the human replacement slot; -916 overrides honored

beats.narration_text: Bula — this is Liam, in for Bear. For Nina Harris' course, Branding and AI, here is the question we are running today: point at any task on your plate and this tool tells you whether you have split it right — AI on the pattern-work, you on the judgment — or handed the machine the part it was never good at. Most AI failures are seam failures. We are building the score that makes the seam explicit.

props.command: Decompose this task: write and approve a crisis statement. Tag each sub-step AI-DOES-BEST or HUMAN-CONDUCTS with rationale. Compute Boondoggle Score (0–100) = share of steps correctly placed. Flag every judgment step that should NOT be delegated to the model.
output: Legal sign-off: HUMAN-CONDUCTS — misplaced if delegated

beats.narration_text: Here is what breaks without the score. You tell Claude to write your brand's crisis statement. It produces something that sounds reasonable. You paste it into the email, send it, and own the legal exposure on a statement you never actually read. The machine did pattern-work fine — it drafted from examples in its training. The seam failure was handing it the approval. Most AI failures are not model failures. They are seam failures.

props.sparkLine: Most AI failures are seam failures.

beats.narration_text: The theory from Chapter Ninety-Seven. The Boondoggling Argument: the seam between AI and human is the design decision that determines whether the tool helps or harms. Two categories of sub-steps: AI-does-best — pattern recognition, high-volume drafting, reformatting, retrieval — and human-conducts — judgment, taste, causal reasoning, accountable sign-off. The Boondoggle Score is a fraction: steps correctly placed over total steps. A score of one hundred means every step is with the right owner. A score below fifty means you have handed judgment to the machine or pattern-work to yourself.

Honesty about what the score does not do. A well-placed seam still needs the human to actually exercise the judgment at the gate. The score is not a safety guarantee — it is an architecture diagnostic. And the final call on whether a step is genuinely a judgment step belongs to the human, not to the model that decomposed the task.

artifactLines: A well-placed seam still needs the human to exercise the judgment at the gate.
The score is a diagnostic, not a safety guarantee.
The final call on whether a step is a judgment step belongs to the human.
The tool decomposes — the human decides which steps are genuinely theirs.

beats.narration_text: Let us recap with Claude. Boondoggle Score Calculator. Four lines.

artifactLines: Most AI failures are seam failures — the machine did pattern-work fine.
Two categories: AI-does-best (pattern, volume, retrieval) and human-conducts (judgment, sign-off).
The Boondoggle Score is the fraction of steps correctly placed between the two owners.
The score names where judgment has been handed to the machine — before the failure happens.

beats.narration_text: Your turn. Take this prompt into your Claude session: Decompose this task into concrete sub-steps: describe a real task from your own work. For each step, tag it AI-DOES-BEST — pattern recognition, high-volume drafting, reformatting, retrieval — or HUMAN-CONDUCTS — judgment, taste, causal reasoning, accountable sign-off — with a one-line reason. Then compute a Boondoggle Score from zero to one hundred, where the score is the share of steps whose current owner matches the correct owner. List every judgment step that should NOT be delegated to the model. Pick a task you are actually running on AI assistance right now. The score will show you where the seam is wrong — and specifically which judgment calls you are currently handing to the machine.

props.command: Decompose this task: [MY TASK]. Tag each sub-step AI-DOES-BEST or HUMAN-CONDUCTS with rationale. Compute Boondoggle Score (0–100) = share of steps correctly placed. List every judgment step that should NOT be delegated to the model.

beats.narration_text: That's the short version. The full video also covers The centerpiece: the calculator's output… — watch Build a Boondoggle Score Calculator with Claude for the whole story. The link is right below.
